title: Present Simple
explanation_en: Used for habits, routines, facts, and general truths. Add -s/-es for he/she/it.
explanation_es: Se usa para hábitos, rutinas, hechos y verdades generales. Añade -s/-es para he/she/it.
negative: I do not (don't) work on Sundays.
question: Do you work here?
common_error: He work → WRONG. He works → CORRECT
tip_es: Recuerda: en tercera persona singular (he/she/it) siempre añades -s/-es al verbo.

title: Present Continuous
explanation_en: Used for actions happening right now or temporary situations.
explanation_es: Se usa para acciones que ocurren en este momento o situaciones temporales.
question: Are you listening?
common_error: She studys → WRONG. She is studying → CORRECT
tip_es: El verbo en -ing nunca va solo, siempre necesita am/is/are delante.

title: Past Simple
explanation_en: Used for completed actions at a specific time in the past.
explanation_es: Se usa para acciones completadas en un momento específico del pasado.
structure: Subject + verb (past form) / did + verb (base form) for questions/negatives
negative: I did not (didn't) go to school yesterday.
question: Did you see the film?
negative_es: No fui a la escuela ayer.
common_error: I did went → WRONG. I went → CORRECT
tip_es: Para negaciones y preguntas en pasado simple, usa DID + infinitivo sin -to.

title: Present Perfect
explanation_en: Connects a past action to the present. Used for experiences, recent events, and unfinished time periods.
explanation_es: Conecta una acción pasada con el presente. Se usa para experiencias, eventos recientes y períodos de tiempo no terminados.
question: Have you ever tried sushi?
common_error: I have went → WRONG. I have gone → CORRECT
tip_es: SINCE = desde un punto en el tiempo (since 2020). FOR = durante un período (for three years).

title: Future with WILL
explanation_en: Used for predictions, spontaneous decisions, promises, and offers.
explanation_es: Se usa para predicciones, decisiones espontáneas, promesas y ofrecimientos.
question: Will you help me?
common_error: She will goes → WRONG. She will go → CORRECT
tip_es: Después de WILL el verbo siempre va en forma base, nunca con -s o -ing.

title: Future with GOING TO
explanation_en: Used for plans and intentions, or predictions based on evidence.
explanation_es: Se usa para planes e intenciones, o predicciones basadas en evidencia.
structure: Subject + am/is/are + going to + verb (base form)
affirmative: We are going to travel this summer.
negative: He is not going to quit.
question: Are you going to study tonight?
common_error: I'm going to studying → WRONG. I'm going to study → CORRECT
tip_es: WILL = decisión en el momento / GOING TO = plan ya decidido con anticipación.

title: Past Continuous
explanation_en: Used for actions in progress at a specific moment in the past, or background context for another action.
explanation_es: Se usa para acciones en progreso en un momento específico del pasado, o como contexto de fondo para otra acción.
affirmative: I was reading when she called.
question: What were you doing at 8pm?
affirmative_es: Yo estaba leyendo cuando ella llamó.
common_error: I was read → WRONG. I was reading → CORRECT
tip_es: WHILE + past continuous / WHEN + past simple. La acción más larga va con WHILE.

title: First Conditional
explanation_en: Used for real or possible future conditions and their likely results.
explanation_es: Se usa para condiciones reales o posibles en el futuro y sus resultados probables.
structure: If + present simple, will + verb (base form)
affirmative: If it rains, I will stay home.
negative: If you don't study, you won't pass.
question: What will you do if you win?
affirmative_es: Si llueve, me quedaré en casa.
common_error: If it will rain → WRONG. If it rains → CORRECT
tip_es: En la cláusula con IF nunca se usa WILL. Usa el presente simple.

title: Second Conditional
explanation_en: Used for hypothetical or imaginary situations in the present or future.
explanation_es: Se usa para situaciones hipotéticas o imaginarias en el presente o futuro.
structure: If + past simple, would + verb (base form)
affirmative: If I had more money, I would travel.
negative: If she wasn't busy, she would help.
question: What would you do if you were me?
negative_es: Si ella no estuviera ocupada, ayudaría.
common_error: If I would have → WRONG. If I had → CORRECT
tip_es: If I WERE you es la forma correcta (no 'was') en inglés formal.

title: Third Conditional
explanation_en: Used for hypothetical situations in the past — things that did NOT happen.
explanation_es: Se usa para situaciones hipotéticas en el pasado — cosas que NO ocurrieron.
structure: If + past perfect, would have + past participle
affirmative: If she had studied, she would have passed.
negative: If he hadn't lied, it wouldn't have happened.
question: What would have happened if you had stayed?
affirmative_es: Si ella hubiera estudiado, habría pasado.
negative_es: Si él no hubiera mentido, no habría pasado.
question_es: ¿Qué habría pasado si te hubieras quedado?
common_error: If I would have gone → WRONG. If I had gone → CORRECT
tip_es: El tercer condicional expresa arrepentimiento o situaciones alternativas del pasado.

title: Passive Voice
explanation_en: Shifts focus from the doer to the action or the receiver. The doer may be omitted or added with 'by'.
explanation_es: Desplaza el foco del actor a la acción o al receptor. El actor puede omitirse o añadirse con 'by'.
affirmative: The book was written by Tolkien.
negative: The report has not been submitted.
question: Was the door locked?
affirmative_es: El libro fue escrito por Tolkien.
negative_es: El informe no ha sido entregado.
common_error: The book was wrote → WRONG. The book was written → CORRECT
tip_es: Se usa mucho en noticias, ciencia y escritura formal para no mencionar quién hace la acción.

title: Relative Clauses
explanation_en: Defines or gives extra information about a noun. Defining (no commas) vs non-defining (with commas).
explanation_es: Define o añade información sobre un sustantivo. Definidas (sin comas) vs no definidas (con comas).
affirmative: The woman who called is my boss.
negative: The book that I lost was expensive.
question: Do you know the place where they met?
affirmative_es: La mujer que llamó es mi jefa.
negative_es: El libro que perdí era caro.
common_error: The man which called → WRONG. The man who called → CORRECT
tip_es: WHO para personas, WHICH para cosas, THAT puede usarse para ambos en cláusulas definidas.

title: Reported Speech
explanation_en: Used to report what someone said without quoting them directly. Tenses move back one step.
explanation_es: Se usa para reportar lo que alguien dijo sin citarlo directamente. Los tiempos verbales retroceden un paso.
negative: He told me he hadn't eaten.
question: She asked if I was coming.
negative_es: Él me dijo que no había comido.
common_error: She said me → WRONG. She told me / She said (to me) → CORRECT
tip_es: SAY no lleva objeto directo de persona. TELL siempre lleva a quién (told me, told her).

title: Subjunctive Mood
explanation_en: Expresses wishes, demands, suggestions, or hypothetical situations. Common after: suggest, recommend, insist, require, demand, propose.
explanation_es: Expresa deseos, exigencias, sugerencias o situaciones hipotéticas. Común después de: suggest, recommend, insist, require, demand.
structure: verb + that + subject + base infinitive (no -s for 3rd person)
affirmative: I suggest that he apply immediately.
negative: It is essential that she not be late.
question: Who recommended that we hire him?
affirmative_es: Sugiero que él se postule de inmediato.
negative_es: Es esencial que ella no llegue tarde.
common_error: I suggest that he applies → WRONG (formal). I suggest that he apply → CORRECT
tip_es: En inglés americano el subjuntivo es más común que en inglés británico.

title: Inversion for Emphasis
explanation_en: Inverted word order is used for emphasis, especially after negative adverbials. Common in formal and literary English.
explanation_es: El orden invertido se usa para dar énfasis, especialmente después de adverbios negativos. Común en inglés formal y literario.
structure: Negative adverb + auxiliary + subject + main verb
affirmative: Never have I seen such courage.
negative: Not only did she win, but she broke the record.
question: Rarely does he make mistakes.
negative_es: No solo ganó, sino que rompió el récord.
common_error: Never I have seen → WRONG. Never have I seen → CORRECT
tip_es: La inversión da un tono muy formal o dramático al texto. Es frecuente en literatura y discursos.
